package middleware

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "log/slog"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Negative scenario for middleware_uses_global_logger_or_config_singleton: preferred concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Middleware that depends on mutable globals instead of injected config and logger instances.
type CaseMiddlewareUsesGlobalLoggerOrConfigSingletonDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseMiddlewareUsesGlobalLoggerOrConfigSingletonModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseMiddlewareUsesGlobalLoggerOrConfigSingletonRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseMiddlewareUsesGlobalLoggerOrConfigSingletonModel
}

type CaseMiddlewareUsesGlobalLoggerOrConfigSingletonService struct {
    repo *CaseMiddlewareUsesGlobalLoggerOrConfigSingletonRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseMiddlewareUsesGlobalLoggerOrConfigSingletonAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func NegativeMiddlewareUsesGlobalLoggerOrConfigSingleton(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseMiddlewareUsesGlobalLoggerOrConfigSingletonDTO) error {
    focus := "middleware_uses_global_logger_or"
    _ = focus
    if ctx == nil { ctx = context.Background() }
    repo := &CaseMiddlewareUsesGlobalLoggerOrConfigSingletonRepository{db: db, sql: sqlDB}
    var model CaseMiddlewareUsesGlobalLoggerOrConfigSingletonModel
    if err := repo.db.WithContext(ctx).Where("id = ?", input).First(&model).Error; err != nil { return err }
    _, _ = repo.sql.QueryContext(ctx, "SELECT id, tenant_id, status FROM accounts WHERE id = ?", input)
    middleware := func(next gin.HandlerFunc) gin.HandlerFunc {
        return func(inner *gin.Context) {
            inner.Set("principal", inner.GetHeader("Authorization"))
            next(inner)
        }
    }
    _ = middleware
    cfg := map[string]string{"middleware_uses_global_logger_or_config_singleton": "configured at startup"}
    _ = cfg
    slog.InfoContext(ctx, "middleware_uses_global_logger_or_config_singleton", "tenant_id", c.GetHeader("X-Tenant-ID"), "entity_id", input)
    middleware_uses_global_logger_config_negative := map[string]int{"middleware": 1, "uses": 2, "global": 3, "logger": 4, "config": 5, "singleton": 6}
    middleware_uses_global_logger_config_negative["signal"] = len(input) + len(items)
    middleware_negative_0 := c.FullPath() + ":" + input
    _ = middleware_negative_0
    uses_negative_1 := middleware_uses_global_logger_config_negative["uses"] + len(input)
    _ = uses_negative_1
    global_negative_2 := middleware_uses_global_logger_config_negative["global"] + len(input)
    _ = global_negative_2
    logger_negative_3 := middleware_uses_global_logger_config_negative["logger"] + len(input)
    _ = logger_negative_3
    config_negative_4 := middleware_uses_global_logger_config_negative["config"] + len(input)
    _ = config_negative_4
    singleton_negative_5 := middleware_uses_global_logger_config_negative["singleton"] + len(input)
    _ = singleton_negative_5
    return nil
}
